content:
Summary: This blog will introduce a convenient extension named ‘Color Highlight’ to automatically display RGB colors in Visual Studio Code. First, open the ‘Visual Studio Code’, then search ‘Color Highlight’ in the ‘extensions’, and install it.
Figure 1 Second, click the “gear-like button” and click the “Extension Settings”.
Figure 2 Third, check the option inside the red box, showed in Figure 3.
Figure 3 Finally, you can enjoy your RGB color during writing in Visual Studio Code!
Figure 4
summary: Summary: This blog will introduce a convenient extension named ‘Color Highlight’ to automatically display RGB colors in Visual Studio Code.
title: Automatically Display RGB Colors in VS Code

content: Welcome to my website! My name is Haiyan Kong (孔海燕). I am currently a research assistant at the Institute of Psychology, Chinese Academy Sciences. Before that, I graduated with the Master of Philosophy degree in Psychology from Nanjing Normal University of China in 2023.
title: Haiyan Kong

content:
Summary: This blog will introduce a free and convenient tool to change files or folder used by something you do not know. When you want to delete, cut, rename, or move some files or folder on your Windows computer, but the pop-up will alert you that “File In Use” or “Folder In Use”.
You can use the tool named LockHunter to solve this error. It is totally free and very convenient.
Download it from the website https://lockhunter.com/, and install it anywhere you want in your computer, then open it.
Figure 1 Just drag the file or folder you want to unlock into the window like Figure 1, then you will see the window like Figure 2. It shows the detailed information on the process.
Figure 2 Just click the “Unlock It!” or “Delete It!” as you want.
Furthermore, it deletes files into the recycle bin, so you may restore them if deleted by mistake
summary: Summary: This blog will introduce a free and convenient tool to change files or folder used by something you do not know.
title: Resolve “File In Use” or “Folder In Use”

content: Summary: Sync Obsidian note between Windows and Android for free by Foldersync + WebDAV. Step1: Prepare the followings Obsidian on Windows and Android FolderSync on Android Cloud service account Note: II will use WebDAV by Nutstore for example, you can use any other Cloud service you like. Step2: Authorize Nutstore applications Open the Nutstore - Preferences - Manage third-party apps. Click the “Acquire current key”, and then fill a name in the blank. Copy the key. Note: we will use the information in Figure 1. Figure 1 Step3: Set authorization in FolderSync Click the “Accounts” in FolderSync and “Add account” Then, choose the “WebDav” for the account type Fill the blank in Figure 2 using the information in Figure 1 Figure 2 Step4: Sync your Obsidian folder to Nutstore in Windows Sync your Obsidian folder to Nutstore in Windows like Figure 3. Then you will see the folder in nutstore. Figure 3 Step5: Build a folder to sync the Obsidian folder in Windows Build a special folder in your smartphone. Step6: Sync the folders you set in step5 and step4 Back to FolderSync - “account”. Choose “Folderpair”. Follow the settings in Figure 4. You can refer my example settings in Figure 5. Figure 4 Figure 5 Step7: Set basic items in FolderSync Set basic items in FolderSync as you like. Step8: Begin the synchronization！ Choose the “Sync” after the settings in Step7, click “Sync” in Figure 6, and wait for a while. If the blue box turn to green like Figure 7, your synchronization is successful. You can also click the little icon in Figure 7, where shows synchronization details. Figure 6 Figure 7 Step9: Open the synchronization folder in the Android Obsidian Follow the settings in Figure 8 to Figure 9. Then you can see all the files in your smartphone! Figure 8 Figure 9 Reference How to Sync Your Obsidian Vault Across Devices for Free Sync Obsidian note between windows and android on google drive Sync Mobile app through Google Drive (Android-Windows10) Setting up Git syncing for Obsidian on Android To Windows + iOS users, how do you sync Obsidian with Obsidian mobile without paying? Jianguoyun Website obsidian移动端库同步：FolderSync搭配坚果云的第三方WebDAV Obsidian&FolderSync 创作格外丝滑
summary: Summary: Sync Obsidian note between Windows and Android for free by Foldersync + WebDAV.
title: Sync Obsidian to Android

content:
Summary: Zotero installation and settings. Recording for sharing and also reference for myself if re-install new Zotero in future. Installation Download the package in the Zotero website (This version is Zotero 7 beta, but if you want to install Zotero 6, you can click here) and install.
Basic Settings Click the Settings(Zotero 6 is Preference) in Edit, and then:
General My settings showed in Figure 1.
Figure 1.General settings Figure 1.1.Filename Format settings {{ year suffix }}{{ authors max="1" name="family" join="_" suffix="_" case="hyphen" }}{{ title truncate="30" case="hyphen" }} Sync My settings showed in Figure 2, I used InfiniCLOUD for cloud store items and pdf files in my Zotero. The free plan in InfiniCLOUD (Figure 2.1) is enough for these literature files storage (I think).
Figure 2.Sync settings Figure 2.1.InfiniCLOUD Export My settings showed in Figure 3.
Figure 3.Export settings Cite My settings showed in Figure 4.
Figure 4.Cite settings Advanced My settings showed in Figure 5 & 6.
Note: “Data Dictionary Location” means the place in your computer you want to store your Zotero data (everything in Zotero, such as items info, items pdf attachments, other attachments, and …).
Figure 5.Advanced settings1 Figure 6.Advanced settings2 Advanced Settings: Add-on Click the Add-on in Tools. Figure 7 showed the add-on extensions I used. All of them are excellent add-on extensions!
Note: Figure 7.1 recorded the Better BibTex Settings in my Zotero.
Figure 7.Add-on Figure 7.1.Better BibTex Settings [year][auth:lower]'_'[shorttitle1_0:lower] year + auth(n=0,m=1,creator="*",initials=false).fold.lower + "'_'" + shorttitle(1,0).lower Basic function Export references
Method 1: After selecting an item, click Edit Figure 8 ​	Copy citation (复制引注)
​	(Beffel & Nuttall, 2020)
​	copy reference list (复制参考文献表)
​	Beffel, J. H., & Nuttall, A. K. (2020). Influences of parentification and benefit finding on prosocial behavior among typically developing siblings of individuals with autism spectrum disorder. Research in Developmental Disabilities, 104, 103694. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.ridd.2020.103694
Method 2: After selecting an item, right click Figure 9 Figure 10 Delete local files while deleting attachments
Figure 11 Show items from sub-collections
Figure 12 Reference Awesome Zotero Plugins Zotero 插件下载 Update: 29 February 2024
title: Zotero installation and settings

content:
Summary: The way to I used updates R and Rstudio in Windows. Step 1: Update R version in R gui Run the following code in R gui.
install.packages("installr") library(installr) require(installr) updateR() During updating, you will be prompted whether need to copy the R package installed in the old version to the new version (such as Figure 1 and Figure 2), pay attention to choose according to your needs.
Figure 1 Figure 2 Step 2: Update R in Rstudio Open Rstudio, click Tools-Global Options, choose General. If the R version is the newest, means you complete the updating. If not, click Change, you will see figure 4, then click “choose a special version of R, choose the newest one.
Figure 3 Figure 4 Step 3: Update Rstudio Click Help in Rstudio, and choose Check for Update. It will be directly linked to the official website of Rstudio, directly downloaded and installed.
Figure 5 In addition, you can also check your R version by the following code.
# Check version R version Step 4: Update Rpackages in Rstudio In Tool, choose Check for Package Updates. If you get figure 7, means all your packages are newest version.
Figure 6 Figure 7 Otherwise, you can also use code to update all your R packages in Rstudio.
# check packages old.packages() # update all the packages update.packages()
summary: Summary: The way to I used updates R and Rstudio in Windows.
title: Update R and Rstudio in Windows

content:
Summary: If you want to rollback project files to where they were before the last commit in GitHub Desktop, you can follow this blog. Step 1
Choose the project you want to rollback in GitHub Desktop, as presented in Figure 1.
Figure 1 Step 2
Click the History in Figure 2, find the newest commit, and click right mouse button, and choose Revert changes in commit.
Figure 2 Step 3
If you completed step 2 successfully, you will see the changes like Figure 3 green box. These mean the project creates a new “commit” record rather than being restored immediately.
Click the Changes.
Figure 3 Step4
Click the Push origin. Then you will get your original project before the committed.
Figure 4 Reference
Rollback uncommitted changes in GitHub Desktop (or Github for Windows)
GitHub Desktop rollback
summary: Summary: If you want to rollback project files to where they were before the last commit in GitHub Desktop, you can follow this blog.
title: Rollback in GitHub Desktop

content:
Basic Grammar Front style \\textbf{bold} \\underline{underline} \\usepackage{ulem} \\uuline{doubleunderline} \\sout{deleteline1} \\xout{deleteline2} \\uwave{waveline} Split \\noindent\\hfil$*$\\hfil$*$\\hfil$*$\\hfil$*$\\hfil$*$\\hfil$*$\\hfil$*$\\hfil
\\rule[10pt]{7.5cm}{0.05em}
Spcae Regular Space In text, use two consecutive spaces or \ to insert a regular space. Example: This is a regular\ space.
Specified Length Space Use \hspace{length} command to insert a space of specified length. Example: \hspace{1cm} will insert a space of 1 centimeter in length.
Flexible Space Use \hfill to insert a flexible space within a line, ensuring even distribution of text. Example: Left\hfill Right will insert a flexible space between “Left” and “Right”.
Non-Breakable Space Use the ~ symbol to insert a non-breakable space that won’t be separated at line breaks. Example: This is a non-breakable~space.
Inserting Multiple Spaces If you need to insert multiple consecutive spaces, you can repeat \ or use the \hspace{length} command. Example: This is\ \ \ \ multiple consecutive spaces. Or \hspace{2cm}.
To do list LaTeX - Kuroko
Tips for LaTeX3 Beginners
Update: 18 March 2024
title: My LaTex Learning Note

content:
Summary:
Convert the data recorded by SHIMADZU (data.txt) to HOMER readable format (data.nirs). Also on Github: HaiyanKong/tutorial_fNIRStxt2nirs Step1: Prepare the followings MATLAB2013b
homer2
Shimadzu2nirs
Step2: Set the path Set homer2 in the path of MATLAB2013b.
Figure 1 Step3: Open the SDgui Set your work path at D:\toolbox\homer2\PACKAGES\SDgui (change the path in the computer)
Run SDgui.m in MATLAB2013b.
Figure 2 Next, a prompt will appear for you to select a file. Simply click “Cancel” to proceed. Figure 3 Then you will see this. Figure 4 Step4: Create the SD File Base on your optodes arrangement to fill in the blank in the sources and detectors. Note: z is always 0. Figure 5 When you complete setting the sources and detectors , then click the number, you will get the channel. Figure 6 Figure 7 After setting channels, and you need to fill in the “Lambda” based on your data acquiring device. Figure 8 Provide a name for the SD file, and let’s call it “test.SD,” including the “.SD” suffix. Figure 9 Click the ‘‘Save", and in the new window, you need to choose the “units”, here is “mm” Figure 10 Your SD file is saved successfully ! Just close the SDgui. Figure 11 Step5: Convert! Put the data need to convert (.txt), 3 files from Shimadzu2nirs.zip and test.SD created in step 4 together, and set the MABLAB work path to them. Figure 12 Run the Shimadzu2nirs.m. Figure 13 Then you will get the .nirs data file ! Figure 14
title: Convert fNIRS data.txt to data.nirs

content:
Summary: This blog will present a solution to address the MATLAB warning: “Variable cannot be saved to a MAT-file with a version older than 7.3.” When using MATLAB to save variables, you might encounter a warning message similar to the one below:
Warning: The variable 'a' cannot be saved to a MAT-file with a version older than 7.3. To resolve this issue, you can follow these steps:
Open MATLAB and click on Preferences.
Navigate to the General tab and select MAT-Files.
Choose the first option: “MATLAB Version 7.3 or later (save -v7.3).”
Figure 1
summary: Summary: This blog will present a solution to address the MATLAB warning: “Variable cannot be saved to a MAT-file with a version older than 7.
title: MATLAB Warning: Saving Variables to Older MAT-File Versions
